mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust and expanding economy, leading to upward revisions of corporate earnings forecasts and thus positive forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month change in the Consumer Price Index exceeds a critical threshold, it signals accelerating inflation that forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a tight labor market (low unemployment) and a rising federal funds rate signals an overheating, late-cycle economy. This joint condition raises investor fears of an impending policy-induced recession, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below its 24-month moving average and FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 3 months.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate for predicting negative 60-day returns does not exceed the baseline probability of a negative return by a statistically significant margin (chi-squared test, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, strong retail sales growth is interpreted negatively by markets, as it increases the likelihood of further aggressive rate hikes to curb demand-pull inflation. This leads to lower equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased over the prior 6 months.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the correlation between month-over-month RSAFS growth and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals a significant slowdown in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, leading to immediate downward revisions in corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly lower than the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts indicates strong consumer confidence and robust forward-looking demand for big-ticket items, which translates into higher expected economic growth and positive equity market performance over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the effective federal funds rate signals a hawkish monetary stance, immediately increasing the discount rate for near-term cash flows and causing a negative repricing of equities over a very short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of slowing real GDP growth and a low unemployment rate indicates falling labor productivity and rising unit labor costs. This stagflationary signal compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: A strategy of shorting the S&P 500 for 60 days when the trigger conditions are met fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.
